host.kernel_cmdline: virtme_hostname=virtme-ng nr_open=1048576 virtme_initmount0=home/yunwei37/workspace/bpf-benchmark virtme_rw_overlay0=/etc virtme_rw_overlay1=/lib virtme_rw_overlay2=/home virtme_rw_overlay3=/opt virtme_rw_overlay4=/srv virtme_rw_overlay5=/usr virtme_rw_overlay6=/var virtme_rw_overlay7=/tmp console=ttyS0 earlyprintk=serial,ttyS0,115200 quiet loglevel=0 panic=-1 virtme.exec=`` virtme_chdir=home/yunwei37/workspace/bpf-benchmark rootfstype=9p rootflags=version=9p2000.L,trans=virtio,access=any raid=noautodetect ro quiet loglevel=1 init=/home/yunwei37/.local/lib/python3.12/site-packages/virtme/guest/bin/virtme-ng-init

benchmarks.name: rotate64_hash
benchmarks.description: SipHash-like hash using 64-bit rotations for ROTATE directive coverage
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense 64-bit rotations should exercise the 4-insn rotate JIT directive pattern

benchmarks.name: rotate_dense
benchmarks.description: Pure register-resident ARX rounds over native-loaded seed words
benchmarks.hypothesis: Isolates ROTATE lowering by keeping the hot path entirely in registers after seed loads

benchmarks.name: cmov_dense
benchmarks.description: 32 inlined compare/select sites over staged u64 arrays
benchmarks.hypothesis: Many inlined select sites should magnify any kernel JIT benefit from branch-to-cmov lowering

benchmarks.name: mixed_alu_mem
benchmarks.description: Interleaved ALU and irregular memory operations over a shared ring
benchmarks.hypothesis: Alternating compute and memory should separate scalar scheduling gains from pure load bandwidth

benchmarks.name: bpf_call_chain
benchmarks.description: Chain of 3 __noinline BPF-to-BPF subprogram calls per loop iteration
benchmarks.hypothesis: Subprogram call overhead should differ between kernel callee-saved convention and LLVM direct calls

benchmarks.name: large_mixed_1000
benchmarks.description: Very large mixed straight-line body targeting roughly 1000 BPF instructions
benchmarks.hypothesis: Very large bodies should amplify backend scalability differences hidden by tiny kernels

benchmarks.name: large_mixed_500
benchmarks.description: Large mixed straight-line body targeting roughly 500 BPF instructions
benchmarks.hypothesis: Larger hot bodies should begin to surface I-cache and allocator scalability effects

benchmarks.name: bounds_ladder
benchmarks.description: Record parsing with dense layered bounds checks
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense bounds checks should stress compare-heavy code generation more than raw memory bandwidth

benchmarks.name: log2_fold
benchmarks.description: Fold log2 over generated u64 input
benchmarks.hypothesis: Mixes compares and bit tests with modest control-flow

benchmarks.name: switch_dispatch
benchmarks.description: Dense switch dispatch over generated 6-bit values
benchmarks.hypothesis: Dense dispatch should reflect jump-table or compare-ladder lowering quality

benchmarks.name: branch_dense
benchmarks.description: High-density conditional branch mesh over generated u32 inputs
benchmarks.hypothesis: Many short conditional paths should magnify branch lowering and layout quality

benchmarks.name: multi_acc_8
benchmarks.description: Eight-lane accumulator kernel with cross-lane mixing
benchmarks.hypothesis: Higher lane count should trade ILP gains against register pressure

benchmarks.name: multi_acc_4
benchmarks.description: Four-lane accumulator kernel with cross-lane mixing
benchmarks.hypothesis: More independent accumulators should reward ILP-friendly code generation

benchmarks.name: cmov_select
benchmarks.description: Repeated single-diamond value selection over staged compare/select arrays
benchmarks.hypothesis: Narrow assignment-only diamonds should expose whether the backend can lower hot selects without branch overhead

benchmarks.name: binary_search
benchmarks.hypothesis: Branch-heavy search should reward stronger branch-oriented code generation
